text.title: Weekly Architecture Review Agenda Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of weekly-arch-review-agenda-template enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: The agenda is fixed and the same 5 items run every week — order may vary, items may not be dropped.
rule.rationale: Fixed agenda removes 'what should we discuss?' overhead and guarantees each lens (decisions, drift, debt, next-week brief).

rule.statement: Every review produces ≥1 decision-log entry; deferral IS a decision and is logged as such.
rule.rationale: Without decision output the meeting becomes status theatre; logged deferrals stay trackable.

rule.statement: Review notes are committed to the repo within 4h of meeting end.
rule.rationale: Late notes lose information; the repo is the audit trail.

rule.statement: Required attendees: architect, two senior engineers from different services, product representative — the meeting cannot proceed without all four.
rule.rationale: Cross-functional triad balances tech/ops/product trade-offs in architectural decisions.

rule.statement: Items that escalate to architectural decisions ALWAYS spawn an ADR via the [[retro-adr-workflow]] before the meeting closes.
rule.rationale: Without ADR spawn the decision evaporates; the ADR is the durable record.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
